summary: Build a diary website named "shinchan" and add a face detection feature (webcam + photo upload).
architectural_notes: Face detection will be implemented in the React frontend (browser-based, e.g., face-api.js) with no backend changes; support both webcam and image upload flows.

existing_features.title: Add Internet Identity sign-in/out to the app and gate diary CRUD features so that only signed-in users can create, view, edit, or delete diary entries.

Implement the diary UI for 'shinchan' with the following screens: (1) Home/Landing page with app name 'shinchan', (2) Entries list with search (by title/content) and filter by date, (3) Entry detail view, (4) Create entry form, (5) Edit entry form, (6) Delete confirmation.
Wire the frontend to the backend using React Query for all diary operations (list/get/create/update/delete), including loading states, error handling, and success feedback.
Apply a cohesive visual theme for the 'shinchan' diary app (consistent colors, typography, spacing, and component styling) using Tailwind + shadcn components; avoid a blue/purple primary palette.

Add generated static brand images to the frontend and display them in the UI (e.g., logo in header and subtle background/hero visual on landing page). Store generated assets under frontend/public/assets/generated and reference them directly from the frontend (no backend image routing).
Add a new authenticated Face Detector page that supports both (1) live webcam face detection and (2) photo upload face detection, running entirely in the browser (no server-side processing).
Integrate a browser-based face detection library (e.g., face-api.js) and add required model assets as static files so the Face Detector page works in production builds.
Add an entry point to reach the Face Detector page from the existing app UI (navigation) using English user-facing labels.

new_feature_requests.summary: Create the core diary data model and persistence in the Motoko single-actor backend to store diary entries per authenticated user (by caller principal). Each entry must include: id, createdAt, updatedAt, entryDate (user-selected date), title, and content.
Add face detection to the shinchan diary app supporting both webcam live detection and photo upload detection (frontend-based, e.g., face-api.js).
